bids.reasoning: At the opening (t=0.0), I am targeting my maximum-utility bid because there is no time pressure to concede yet. There are no opponent offers or messages to infer their priorities, so I will start with my best values on all issues, including issueD where I am indifferent due to zero weight.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making modest concessions rather than reacting strongly to the opponent’s aggressive message. Their counteroffer gives me about 0.416 utility and suggests they may prefer issueA:valueA and issueD:valueA, so I concede there first because issueA is low weight and issueD has zero weight for me, while holding firm on my high-weight issues B, E, and F.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.85 while making a visible concession from my maximum. The opponent has not improved their offers to me and their messages are pressure rather than evidence of flexibility, so I am conceding mainly on lower-weight issues while holding firm on issueF, issueE, and issueB.

It is still very early, so I am targeting a high utility bid while making a visible concession on lower-weight issues rather than giving up issueB or issueF, which matter much more to me. The opponent’s offers have not improved for my utility and their messages focus on reducing valueC positions, so I reduce valueC on issueA while keeping my strongest positions on issueB, issueE, and issueF.

At t=0.08 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making visible concessions on lower-weight issues rather than giving up my key issueF, issueB, or issueE positions. The opponent’s offers have not improved for me and their messages are aggressive cheap talk, but they consistently signal pressure against many valueC positions, so I reduce valueC usage on less important issues and keep my strongest weighted preferences.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making visible concessions on lower-weight issues rather than on my most important issueF and issueB. The opponent’s offers consistently push issueC to valueA and complain about issueB/issueF, but their numeric concessions are limited, so I will concede issueC and the zero-weight issueD while holding firm on key issues.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.74 because it is early in the negotiation, but I am making a visible concession on issueB, which the opponent has repeatedly flagged and consistently bids as valueB. The opponent’s latest bid improved for me by moving issueF to valueB, but since issueF is my highest-weight issue, I will hold issueF at valueC for now while conceding on lower/medium-priority areas.

I am targeting a still-strong early-game utility while making a visible concession from my prior bid: I keep my highest-weight issueF and issueE favorable, but concede issueB to valueB and issueC down to valueB. The opponent’s offers remain low utility for me and their messages are aggressive cheap talk, though their repeated focus on issueB/issueF suggests issueB is a better concession point than issueF at this early time.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a solid utility around 0.61 while making visible concessions on lower-weight issues and on issueB. The opponent’s offers remain low-utility for me and they have not reliably conceded, but their bids and messages repeatedly emphasize issueB/issueF; I will hold firm on my highest-weight issueF while offering them movement on issueA, issueC, and issueD.

I am targeting about 0.61 utility, which is still a meaningful concession from my ideal while protecting my highest-weight issueF and issueE. Since time is still early at t=0.18, I am not accepting the opponent’s low-utility proposal, but I am matching most of their latest structure and only holding firm on issueF. Their offers and messages suggest they dislike my valueC positions on issueB/issueF, so I concede issueB while keeping issueF where it matters most to me.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.47 by holding the high-value compatible point issueE=valueA while conceding on issueB and especially issueF compared with my earlier offers. At t=0.20 there is still time, but the opponent has consistently rejected issueF=valueC and repeatedly offered issueC=valueA and issueB=valueB, so I am moving toward a bid they have already signaled they may accept while keeping positive value for me.
